untranslatable_reason: This signal is a position sizing/leverage rule, not a directional entry/exit signal. It requires: (1) calculation of strategy win rate and risk/reward ratio from backtest results (not available as real-time indicators), (2) dynamic computation of half-Kelly leverage, (3) historical worst-case one-period drawdown analysis (meta-level backtest statistic), and (4) maximum acceptable drawdown threshold (AUTHOR_SILENT — not specified). The allowed indicators cannot express these portfolio-level leverage calculations or access backtest performance metrics. This rule belongs in a portfolio management module, not entry/exit conditions.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_QT_DRY_0 is a risk management overlay that dynamically adjusts position size based on Kelly criterion and historical tail risk. Recommend implementing as a separate position_sizing() function that: (a) calculates half_kelly = (win_rate * avg_win - (1 - win_rate) * avg_loss) / avg_win, (b) retrieves max_historical_one_period_loss from backtest equity curve, (c) sets max_leverage = max_acceptable_drawdown / max_historical_one_period_loss, (d) applies leverage = min(half_kelly, max_leverage) to all positions, and (e) rebalances at period end (frequency TBD). Exit on max_tolerable_drawdown breach is a hard stop that must be monitored at portfolio level.
